title: ​The 2026 Red Sox’ run prevention approach has been a disaster out of the gate
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Red Sox have started the 2026 season with a 1-5 record, their worst start since 2019.
Boston's starting rotation has a 5.22 ERA, with key pitchers allowing 11 earned runs over 12 innings.
The Red Sox defense has committed six errors in six games, continuing issues from last season's 116 errors.

title: Stefon Diggs has trial date set in strangulation, assault case
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Stefon Diggs's trial on strangulation and assault charges is set for May 4 in Dedham District Court.
The charges relate to a December incident involving Diggs and his personal chef over an alleged financial dispute.
Diggs pleaded not guilty; his attorney claims the allegations are unsubstantiated and financially motivated.

title: ‘What we had talked about, take that to the grave’: Report reveals details about State Police sergeant who allegedly drove drunk, caused fatal crash
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Massachusetts State Police sergeant Scott Quigley faces charges for allegedly causing a fatal crash while driving drunk in 2023.
An internal report found the Middlesex DA's office and police failed to notify authorities of the crash details promptly.
Quigley's blood alcohol level was allegedly 0.11; post-crash investigations lacked critical specifics, including involvement of a second vehicle.

title: Dorchester woman sentenced for fatal stabbing stemming from online feud
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Dorchester woman Alyssa Partsch, 33, sentenced to 15-20 years for fatally stabbing Jazreanna Sheppard in July 2023.
Partsch pleaded guilty to manslaughter, reducing her original second-degree murder charge in Suffolk County Superior Court.
The fatal incident followed an online feud, with Partsch attacking Sheppard at the Park Street MBTA station.

title: NBA superstar’s message to Connecticut Sun fans: ‘They’ll be alright, they’ve got UConn’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Connecticut Sun's WNBA franchise relocates to Houston, rebranding as the Houston Comets.
NBA star Kevin Durant comments that UConn women's basketball will comfort Connecticut Sun fans.
A proposed $325 million deal to move the Sun to Boston was blocked by the WNBA.

title: Alex Cora declines to elaborate after scratching Carlos Narvaez hours before Red Sox game
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox manager Alex Cora scratched Carlos Narvaez from the lineup before playing the Astros, opting for Connor Wong.
Cora declined to provide details on Narvaez's removal but indicated the decision was in the team's best interest.
Narvaez confirmed the decision was not injury-related, expressing respect for Cora's choice to keep the matter private.

title: Mike Vrabel says Patriots will pick up Christian Gonzalez’s fifth-year option
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Patriots will exercise Christian Gonzalez's fifth-year option, keeping him on the team through the 2027 season.
Gonzalez's base salary will increase to $18.1 million in 2027, up from $2.2 million last season.
Coach Mike Vrabel expressed interest in extending Gonzalez's contract to prevent him from entering free agency in 2028.

title: Elias Lindholm, like the Bruins, is getting hot at the ideal time
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Elias Lindholm has scored five goals and two assists in his last six games for the Boston Bruins.
The Boston Bruins achieved a fourth consecutive victory with a 6-3 win against the Dallas Stars.
David Pastrnak recorded 66 assists this season, contributing three in the latest game against the Stars.

title: For this Everett runner, the Boston Marathon has always been personal
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Michelle Davidson of Everett is running the 2026 Boston Marathon with the Brigham Stepping Strong Foundation.
Davidson was inspired by the city's response to the 2013 Marathon bombings to support trauma care.
The Boston Marathon holds personal significance for Davidson, whose community was directly impacted by the 2013 events.

title: Boston faces budget deficit of nearly $50 million
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston faces a $48.4 million budget deficit attributed to snow removal, police overtime, and health insurance costs.
Mayor Wu's administration delayed hiring and froze spending to address the deficit, expecting to resolve it without using reserves.
Boston Public Schools also facing a separate $53 million deficit, planning potential position cuts despite a $1.7 billion budget.
